forecast-period.text: A trough over eastern and northern SA will continue to move northeastwards clearing the State by Thursday. A ridge of high pressure persists to the south.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers clearing the south. Rain at times about the north, with locally heavy falls possible about the northeast. The chance of a thunderstorm in the north. Maximum temperatures below average. South to southeasterly winds, shifting east to northeasterly in the northeast.

Showers about the northern agricultural area and pastoral districts. Rain at times about the northeast with locally heavy falls possible.The chance of a thunderstorm in the far northeast. Maximum temperatures generally below average, grading to near average in the southeast. South to southeasterly winds, tending easterly in the east.

Showers mainly about the pastoral districts. Possible light showers about western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average, grading to near average about the agricultural area. Generally south to southeasterly winds.

text.p: Showers mainly about the pastoral districts, possibly extending into the northern agricultural area. maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to slightly above average in the south. Generally south to southeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: 2 to 10 mm about the southern agricultural area, 10 to 30 mm about the northern agricultural area, the pastoral districts and the far southwest, increasing to 50 to 100 mm about parts of the north, with isolated falls of 100 to 160 mm.
